seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Flowise + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Flowise + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Flowise is a low-code platform for building customized AI chatbots with drag-and-drop RAG pipelines, agent orchestration, and multi-turn conversations. Integrating Vex guardrails ensures reliability by validating intermediate outputs, detecting hallucinations, and maintaining conversation coherence. This guide shows how to secure your Flowise AI chatbot against common failure modes in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Flowise
architectureOverview.description:
A typical Flowise AI Chatbot follows a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pattern with intent classification and memory management. User input flows through an Intent Classifier Node to route to appropriate chains: conversational (with memory), RAG retrieval, or tool-calling agents. The Vector Store Embeddings node retrieves relevant documents, which are passed to an LLM Chain for grounded response generation, with Conversation Memory nodes maintaining multi-turn context across agent handoffs.

Key components include Custom Tool Nodes for external API calls, Multi-Agent Supervisor patterns for complex routing, and Safety Filter nodes for content moderation. Failures often occur in unvalidated intermediate retrievals, silent tool failures, or context loss during agent transitions, making runtime verification essential for production reliability.

Vex integrates as a callback handler to monitor every node execution, validate outputs against business rules, and provide observability into chain-of-thought reasoning.

risks.name: Unvalidated RAG Retrievals
risks.description: Vector store returns low-relevance documents that degrade response quality
risks.mitigation: Semantic similarity scoring + relevance thresholds via Vex

risks.name: Context Loss in Multi-Turn
risks.description: Memory buffer fails to maintain conversation state between agent handoffs
risks.mitigation: Session state validation and memory reconstruction checks

risks.name: Silent Tool Failures
risks.description: Custom tool nodes fail without error propagation, breaking downstream logic
risks.mitigation: Tool output schema validation and error recovery patterns

risks.name: Hallucination in Final Response
risks.description: LLM generates unsupported claims despite RAG context

implementation.title: Initialize Vex Client in Flowise
implementation.description: Set up Vex SDK with your API key and configure session tracking for chat history

implementation.title: Instrument Chatflow Nodes
implementation.description: Wrap Flowise node outputs with Vex spans for intermediate validation

implementation.title: RAG Relevance Validation

implementation.title: Final Response Guardrails
implementation.description: Comprehensive safety checks before returning response to user

vexIntegration.title: Custom Tool Node Instrumentation

vexIntegration.title: Memory Context Validation
vexIntegration.description: Ensure conversation memory maintains coherence across turns
